brand.name: My Career Journey
brand.tagline: Plan your path after school, one step at a time!

common.comingSoonNote: This page is a placeholder for now — real content will be added here.

copyPrompt.hint: Tip: copy the prompt and paste it into a free AI chatbot (Gemini, ChatGPT, Copilot) for the best answer — a long prompt can get cut short if you only open the Google link. Always double-check exam dates and fees on the official websites.

home.heroTitle: What do you want to do after school? 🤔
home.heroSubtitle: Get a step-by-step plan towards the career you want — colleges, exams, costs and timelines, all in one place!
home.feature1Body: Every milestone in order — which class, which exam, which year.
home.feature2Body: Compare options and filter them by cost: free, low, mid or high.
home.feature3Body: Check specific links to the official sites to confirm.
home.disclaimer: Dates, fees and eligibility change every year. Always confirm on official links before acting!

generatingChat.promptTitle: Get your plan from a free AI tool instead
generatingChat.promptLead: Copy this ready-made prompt and paste it into any free AI chatbot — or search it on Google. Your career and class are already filled in.
generatingChat.fallbackLead: No need to wait — you can still plan this yourself. Our step-by-step guide shows how to research any career and check the dates, fees, and eligibility on the official websites.

journey.candidateBannerTitle: AI-generated — not checked by a person yet
journey.candidateBanner: A computer wrote this plan and no one has reviewed it yet, so some details may be wrong. Always check dates, fees, and exam rules on the official websites before you depend on them.

actions.copyManual: Copy this link to bookmark or share your plan:
actions.copyPromptLead: Paste this into any free AI chatbot, or search it on Google, to regenerate this plan or dig deeper — your career and class are already filled in.

notFound.title: This plan is being refreshed
notFound.body: We couldn't find a saved copy of this plan just now. You can generate a fresh one — it keeps the same shareable link.
notFound.badLink: This plan link looks incomplete. Try starting a new plan.
notFound.error: Couldn't generate a plan right now. Please try again.

cvTemplates.title: CV / Resume Templates
cvTemplates.intro: Free, simple résumé tools for students and first-time job seekers — fill in a quick form to make your CV, or download a blank template to complete yourself. No work experience needed.
cvTemplates.quickIntro: Fill in the boxes and download a finished CV with your details. Choose Word to keep editing it, or PDF to send as-is. Any boxes you leave empty are simply left out.
cvTemplates.privacyNote: Everything you type stays on your device — nothing is uploaded or saved.
cvTemplates.blankIntro: Download a clean, one-page template and fill it in your own way — as an editable Word file, or a plain text file that opens on any phone.

student-no-experience.name: Student CV — no work experience
student-no-experience.guide: Start with your name and how to reach you at the top. You don't need any job experience — school, projects and activities all count. Under Education, put your most recent school or college first. In Projects & activities, list things you've actually done — a class project, a poster you made, helping at a family shop, a sports team — and write one short line on what you did. Under Skills, add the languages you speak and any computer or practical skills. Keep it to one page and use plain, simple words.

first-internship.name: First internship CV
first-internship.guide: This one is for your first internship or part-time role. Put your contact details at the top, then a short two-line About me saying what you're studying and the kind of internship you want — change these two lines for each company you apply to. List your education with the latest first. Under Experience add any work, volunteering or freelance task (even unpaid) with one line on what you did and what changed because of it. Use Projects for course or personal work and Skills for tools and languages. Keep it to one page and save it as a PDF before applying.

cvTemplates.buildersIntro: Prefer to build it on screen? These tools are well-known and free to use:

builders.googleDocs: Free with any Google account — open Google Docs, choose Template gallery, then a Resume template. Download as PDF.
builders.canva: Free templates you fill in by clicking. A free account lets you download your résumé as a PDF.
builders.flowcv: Clean templates you fill in online, with a free PDF download — no payment to export.
builders.indeed: Build a résumé on Indeed and apply to jobs with it. Free to create and download.

cvTemplates.buildersAvoid: Some sites let you build for free but charge to download the PDF — usually after you've spent 30–60 minutes. Before you start, confirm the download is free.

freeResources.title: Free Learning Resources
freeResources.intro: All free. Find what you need by what it's for — tap a filter below.
freeResources.tip: Most of these work on a basic phone. Start with one, not ten.

interviewPrep.title: Interview Prep
interviewPrep.intro: Your first interview feels scary — that's normal. Nobody expects a student to be perfect. They want to see that you're honest, willing to learn, and a little prepared. You can prepare for almost everything below.

method.heading: How to answer most questions
method.intro: When asked “tell me about a time you…”, use 3 steps:
method.exampleQ: “Tell me about a time you worked in a team.”
method.example: Our class had to organise a fundraiser (situation). I was put in charge of collecting and tracking the money (action). We raised ₹8,000 and I made sure every rupee was accounted for (result).
method.outro: That's it. No fancy words needed.

remember.heading: Remember:
remember.body: honest + willing to learn + a little prepared beats “perfect” every time.

findJobs.title: Find Jobs & Internships
findJobs.intro: Not knowing where to look is the biggest hurdle. Here's where to search, how to apply, and how to avoid scams.

scams.heading: Staying safe from scams
scams.intro: Fake “jobs” target young first-time seekers. These rules stop almost all of them:

planItYourself.title: Plan It Yourself
planItYourself.intro: No ready-made match for you yet? Use this blank plan and the short guide below to research any career on your own!
planItYourself.templateIntro: Fill each step in for the career you have in mind.
planItYourself.downloadNote: Print or Save as PDF — your downloaded plan includes everything you've typed below.
planItYourself.monthlyIntro: Block out one or two things to do each month — an exam to register for, a skill to build, someone to talk to.
planItYourself.monthlyPlaceholder: What will you do this month?
templateSteps: The qualification or degree it needs:
The exam(s) involved and roughly when they happen:
Two colleges or institutes near me:
Approximate cost, and where I confirmed it:
planItYourself.guideTitle: How to research any career on your own
guideSteps: Search the career plus "official" to find the governing body or institute.
Find the real website — prefer .gov.in, .org, or the named institute's own domain.
Look for the "admissions" or "apply" page; that's where real dates and fees live.
Sanity-check fees against a second source — if one number looks too low or too high, dig deeper.
Never pay an "agent" for an application you can submit yourself for free.

footer.rights: A free, public-good tool. Always verify specifics on official sites.
